sglang.author: CatherineSue
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation
Current slicing strategy in init_forward_metadata_replay_cuda_graph will create an intermediate tensor of size [total_requests, max_seq_pages].

Modifications

Replace the broadcasting approach that created a full [total_requests, max_seq_pages] intermediate tensor with a two-step indexin

sglang.title: Support `--enable-llama4-multimodal`
sglang.author: ch-wan
sglang.bodyExcerpt:
Motivation

Current implementation for multimodal models has to disable chunked prefill, which may incur OOM for language tasks (e.g., #5212). This PR allows the users to turn off the multimodal function and enable chunked prefill.

vllm.title: [Kernel] support merge_attn_states CUDA kernel, 3x speedup
vllm.author: DefTruth
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
base on vllm/attention/ops/triton_merge_attn_states.py
Use CUDA kernel instead of Triton to minimize CPU overhead. Compared to the Triton kernel, the CUDA kernel implemented in this PR can achieve a maximum speedup of over 3x. @WoosukKwon, End2End performance improved for R1 with PP=3 + TP=8 on L20,

vllm.title: Fix erroneous "model doesn't support compile" warning
vllm.author: zou3519
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
The problem was:

during model init, we use the set_current_vllm_config context manager
if model init fails, the finally block is always executed.
previously, the finally block would always warn that the model doesn't support compile (it assumes the model init doesn't fail)
In this PR, we only execu

vllm.title: [TPU][V1] Make `--disable_chunked_mm_input` mandatory for serving MM models
vllm.author: NickLucche
vllm.bodyExcerpt: This feature was intended to avoid XLA recompiliations when running on TPUs. Make it mandatory.

vllm.title: [Kernel] Support W8A8 channel-wise weights and per-token activations in triton fused_moe_kernel
vllm.author: mgoin
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Adapting in updates to the triton fused_moe kernel to expand the supported quantization formats
This PR enables:

W8A8 FP8 for channel-wise weights and per-token activations
W8A8 INT8 for channel-wise weights and per-token activations
W8A8 INT8 for blocked weights and blocked per-token activation (l

vllm.title: [Doc] Document InternVL3 support
vllm.author: Isotr0py
vllm.bodyExcerpt: As shown in the following figure, InternVL3 retains the same model architecture as InternVL 2.5 and its predecessors, InternVL 1.5 and 2.0, following the "ViT-MLP-LLM" paradigm. In this new version, we integrate a newly incrementally pre-trained InternViT with various pre-trained LLMs, including Int

vllm.title: [Bugfix] handle alignment of encoder_seq_lens in mllama.py
vllm.author: tjohnson31415
vllm.bodyExcerpt: Fix for the crash repro reported in this comment. The bug and fix are pretty similar to #12347 in that the problem arises in a batch of mixed text and image requests leading to the attn metadata having some lists with an element for each sequence and others with an element only for each sequence-wit

vllm.title: Improve configs - `LoadConfig`
vllm.author: hmellor
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follows #16332
Notable changes:

FlexibleArgumentParser has been updated to allow newlines in argument help strings. This enables the LoadFormat.load_format help string to look much nicer.
In the previous PR the config decorator broke the IDE type detection. This is fixed using a TypeVar.
The get_kw

vllm.title: [Frontend] Added chat templates for LLaMa4 pythonic tool calling
vllm.author: yeqcharlotte
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Fixing tool calling related chat templates for llama4. LLaMa4 followed similar pythonic format. Co-authored with @wukaixingxp.
Tool call messages and tool response messages should end with <|eom|>. <|python_start|> and <|python_end|> are not very consistent in the output with current checkpo

Generated with benchmark_moe.py script for the most part, except for bsz=16 where it needed to be tuned further.
Benchmark commands:
VLLM_USE_V1=1 vllm serve meta-llama/Llama-4-Scout-17B-16E-Instruct  --disable-log-requests -tp 8 --max-num-seqs 64 --no-enable-prefix-caching --max_num_batched_tokens=

vllm.title: [BugFix] Handle non-contiguous tensors properly when serializing
vllm.author: njhill
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Follow-on to #13790. Both msgpack and zmq require contiguous tensors so ensure the tensor is converted in all cases.
Also use a msgpack ext for the inline case to avoid a copy on the decode side.

vllm.title: [Feature][V1] Add xgrammar to support minLength, maxLength with test
vllm.author: leon-seidel
vllm.bodyExcerpt: While #15791 implements minLength and maxLength it was not merged yet due to missing a test. This PR adds a test for minLength and maxLength by generating an output with exactly 50 characters.

vllm.title: [Frontend] support matryoshka representation / support embedding API dimensions
vllm.author: noooop
vllm.bodyExcerpt:
Summary
Matryoshka Embeddings or Matryoshka Representation Learning (MRL) is a technique used in training embedding models. It allows user to trade off between performance and cost.
Not all embeddings models support MRL. Changing the output dimension for models that do not support MRL will lead to p
